_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the EGD this patient was referred for.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with hemostasis
procedure.indication: Suspected upper GI bleeding (collateral history of two melena episodes) on chronic self-directed NSAID (naproxen) use; GERD
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy); NPO >8 hours, adequate mucosal visualization

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with endoscopic hemostasis. INDICATION: Suspected upper GI bleeding (collateral melena x2 last week) in the setting of chronic daily naproxen use and GERD; naproxen held since clinic visit. SEDATION: MAC/propofol. FINDINGS: The endoscope was passed without difficulty to the second portion of the duodenum. ESOPHAGUS: Los Angeles Grade A reflux esophagitis with two small mucosal breaks at the GE junction; no varices, no Barrett's segment. STOMACH: Retroflexion normal. A single 12 mm gastric ulcer was identified in the antrum along the lesser curvature with a non-bleeding visible vessel (Forrest IIa). No active spurting or oozing. Endoscopic hemostasis performed: injection of dilute epinephrine (1:10,000) around the ulcer base followed by placement of two hemostatic clips over the visible vessel; adequate hemostasis achieved. Two biopsies taken from the gastric antrum (away from the ulcer) for H. pylori testing; ulcer margins biopsied to exclude malignancy. DUODENUM: Bulb and D2 normal, no ulcer. IMPRESSION: 12 mm antral gastric ulcer, Forrest IIa (non-bleeding visible vessel), treated with epinephrine injection and hemoclips; LA Grade A esophagitis. Likely NSAID-induced ulcer as source of melena. PLAN: High-dose IV PPI transitioning to oral BID, continue naproxen cessation, H. pylori testing, repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm ulcer healing given malignancy exclusion biopsies pending.

findings.finding: Gastric ulcer, 12 mm, Forrest IIa (non-bleeding visible vessel)
Antral mucosa biopsy for H. pylori
LA Grade A reflux esophagitis (two small mucosal breaks)

disposition: Recovered from anesthesia and discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Hemodynamically stable post-procedure. Post-hemostasis and post-biopsy precautions reviewed. Instructed to return/ED for recurrent melena, hematemesis, lightheadedness, or syncope.

followup.recommendation: Review gastric ulcer margin biopsy pathology (malignancy exclusion) and call patient with result
Review H. pylori biopsy result; if positive, prescribe eradication therapy and arrange confirmation of cure
Follow up on same-day/pending CBC (hemoglobin) ordered at pre-procedure visit; assess for anemia requiring iron or transfusion
Continue high-dose PPI (pantoprazole 40 mg BID); do NOT resume naproxen — provide alternative analgesia plan for chronic back pain
Repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm gastric ulcer healing (mandatory given gastric location)

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric ulcer margin biopsies pending to exclude malignancy — gastric ulcers require histologic and endoscopic confirmation of benignity; result must be communicated and acted upon
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path, call patient, ensure malignancy excluded and healing confirmed on repeat EGD

planted_followup_items.description: H. pylori antral biopsy pending — if positive, eradication therapy is required to reduce ulcer recurrence
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review H. pylori result; treat and confirm eradication if positive

planted_followup_items.description: Endoscopically treated gastric ulcer with a Forrest IIa non-bleeding visible vessel in a patient with recent melena — high rebleeding risk; pending CBC/hemoglobin must be reviewed to assess anemia and transfusion need
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review hemoglobin promptly; monitor for rebleeding; escalate/admit if Hgb low or symptomatic

planted_followup_items.description: Naproxen must NOT be resumed — it is the likely ulcer culprit; patient (on no anticoagulant/antiplatelet) needs an alternative back-pain analgesia plan and NSAID-cessation reinforcement, with permanent NSAID-avoidance documented on the reconciled medication list
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document permanent NSAID avoidance on the medication list; coordinate alternative analgesia with PCP

planted_followup_items.description: Repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm gastric ulcer healing (standard of care for gastric ulcers)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule surveillance EGD to document healing and re-biopsy if not healed

planted_followup_items.description: Post-hemostasis rebleeding watch — early rebleeding after epinephrine + clip therapy; patient counseled on return precautions
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure patient understands return precautions; consider next-day check-in call

planted_followup_items.description: LA Grade A reflux esophagitis — continue PPI and reassess reflux control
planted_followup_items.expected_action: continue PPI, reassess GERD symptoms at follow-up

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a little scratchy and I have some mild burning in my upper belly, but nothing bad.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus mild residual dyspepsia; no alarm features
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and mild upper-abdominal burning are expected and self-limited. Continue PPI; return precautions reinforced.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms in a treated-ulcer patient; no red flag, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsy from my stomach? And can I start taking my naproxen again for my back now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Asking about pending pathology and, importantly, about resuming the NSAID that is the likely ulcer culprit
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (~2026-07-27), team will call with result. Counsel that naproxen must NOT be resumed — it likely caused the ulcer; coordinate alternative analgesia (e.g., acetaminophen) with PCP.
contacts.rationale: Benign but a med-timing trap — the patient wants to restart the culprit NSAID. Answerable/counsel from the plan; links to the med_reconciliation NSAID-avoidance item.

contacts.verbatim: My stools have been normal brown and I feel back to myself. Do I really still need another scope in a couple months?
contacts.clinical_signal: No rebleeding (normal brown stools, feels well); asking about the planned surveillance EGD
contacts.expected_action: Reassure that resolution of dark stools is a good sign; explain the repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks is still required to confirm gastric ulcer healing and review pending malignancy-exclusion biopsy. Schedule the surveillance scope.
contacts.rationale: Benign course with an answerable question; links to the repeat_procedure surveillance item. No escalation.
